title: Schema-Driven Channels And Photon Preview Proof Tasks
principles: Build the channel path cleanly from canonical Effect Schemas, Context.Service contracts, and explicit live/memory Layers; do not extract, wrap, migrate, or preserve the current Sendblue implementation.

@bundjil/channel owns one narrow provider-neutral ChannelTransport; @bundjil/sendblue and @bundjil/photon provide it through separate Layers; apps/agent owns Eve, identity, fresh replay, dispatch, and composition.

Decode unknown only at host/provider ingress, decode statically encoded values with Schema.decodeEffect, encode immediately before outward writes, and pass decoded Schema-derived Types between services.
Use a fresh versioned state, replay namespace, and environment namespace; import no legacy state, keys, continuation, typing lifecycle, config namespace, modules, or tests.

One module-edge ManagedRuntime and one runPromise per Eve callback are allowed; nested runtime execution, Effect.orDie, swallowed causes, Object.assign state updates, callback escape hatches, and method-local dependency re-provisioning are acceptance failures.
Photon remains Preview-only and conditional on exact published pins, deterministic serverless lifecycle, signed-webhook/replay proof, isolated resources, and separate authority.
Repository, local, Preview, provider, handset, and Production proof are distinct; protected values and message/identity content never enter evidence.
riskLenses: Effect control flow and helper admission
safe tagged errors and uncertain delivery
globalVerification: Effect language-service diagnostics for changed TypeScript files
narrow scans for forbidden legacy imports, Effect.orDie, swallowed catchCause, Object.assign state writes, nested runPromise, raw callbacks, DTO mirrors, and helper sprawl

tasks.title: Create the active plan and lock the clean replacement boundary
tasks.description: Create the active plan, inventory exact files to replace/remove and external journeys to re-prove, and record the old implementation solely as rejected-pattern and rollback evidence.
implementationNotes: Link the plan from docs/exec-plans/active/README.md and record base commit, branch, environment authority, immutable Production rollback deployment, no-migration decision, ingress drain requirement, and proof boundaries.
List apps/agent/agent/channels/sendblue.ts and every apps/agent/agent/lib/sendblue file as replace/remove targets; do not create a legacy or compatibility directory.
Define new package/app file owners, new environment namespace, fresh state/replay schemas and prefix, exact third-party boundary exceptions, and the removal order.
Identify consumer journeys from current docs without treating old internal tests, status values, state, or replay keys as compatibility requirements.
mandatoryVerification: The plan distinguishes current repository/deployment truth from replacement intent and names the intentional session/replay continuity break.
No live provider/deployment state is read or mutated and no secret/identity content is captured.

Review every planned operation for flat Effect flow, canonical Schema Type/Encoded ownership, named Context.Service operations, explicit Layers, typed pipe error handling, and zero unsafe casts/manual readers/DTO mirrors/helpers.
Run boundary/effect/skill checks, docs formatting/link/frontmatter checks, verification with required ignored synthetic env, diff checks, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: Implementation has an active plan whose source target is a clean replacement, not a compatibility refactor.
Removal, cutover, rollback, and non-authority boundaries are explicit.
evidence: Ownership and call graph: the active plan records the exact three package owners, app-owned service and Eve boundaries, complete legacy removal inventory/order, fresh namespaces, dated Git/deployment rollback identities, intentional continuity break, Photon-only authority, and Vercel/Production stop condition.

Implementation quality: plan review confirms decoded Schema Types between named services, encoded values only at persistence/framework/provider egress, raw Request and SDK primitives confined to exact adapters, explicit live/memory Layers, flat Effect operations, and no compatibility/helper/DTO/manual-reader design.

Verification coverage: jq, routed-link and naming scans, Ultracite formatting, check:boundaries, check:effect-setup, check:skills, Knip, five-workspace typechecks, 246 workspace tests including the 79-test legacy agent baseline, full verification, and git diff --check passed. No provider or deployment operation ran.

tasks.title: Build the canonical channel package and clean app vertical slice
tasks.description: Implement @bundjil/channel plus app-owned Channel, replay, identity, routing, and Eve dispatch services using only deterministic Layers, proving an HTTP-to-Eve-to-outbound journey before either provider exists.
implementationNotes: Export canonical provider/conversation/message/send/presence Schemas with named Type/Encoded aliases, safe tagged errors, ChannelTransport Context.Service, and layerMemory.

Add app-owned ChannelStateV1 with only a conversation snapshot, new replay contracts/prefix through AtomicKeyValueStore, fresh branded continuation routing, ChannelIdentity, ChannelRouter, ChannelReplay, Channel, and request-scoped EveChannelDispatch services.
Project Eve events into one canonical tagged Schema; domain operations return immutable decoded state and explicit outcomes.
Use one test composition root and no import from apps/agent/agent/lib/sendblue.
mandatoryVerification: @effect/vitest proves accepted/ignored ingress, identity rejection, fresh deterministic routing, concurrent duplicate claims, Eve dispatch completion/failure, outbound claim/send outcomes, stateless presence, immutable next state, and encoded snapshot round trips.

All primary operations are named flat Effect.fn/Effect.gen programs with expected catchTag/catchTags/mapError in outer pipes; dependencies come from Context.Service/Layer requirements, never method-local provideService chains.

Assert decodeUnknown occurs only at synthetic host/provider ingress, decodeEffect handles encoded fixtures, encodeEffect owns persistence/framework egress, and there are no unsafe casts/manual readers/DTO mirrors/raw callbacks/trivial helpers.
Run common/agent focused checks, boundary/effect/skill checks, Effect diagnostics, negative legacy-pattern scans, verification, diff checks, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: A full clean journey passes entirely through memory Layers and canonical contracts.
The slice contains no provider or legacy dependency.
evidence: Ownership and call graph: @bundjil/channel owns the nominal direct-text transport Schemas, safe common tagged errors, service, and memory Layer; apps/agent owns Channel identity, deterministic fresh routing, atomic replay, orchestration, immutable state/events, and Eve dispatch. Imports point inward to @bundjil/channel, @bundjil/eve, and @bundjil/store only; no provider or legacy module enters the slice.

Implementation quality: public package values use branded Schema-derived Types and explicit Encoded aliases; app persistence decodes canonical store values and uses AtomicKeyValueStore.transact; named Context.Service operations and explicit Layers form one dependency graph; primary Effects are flat; no unsafe assertion, DTO mirror, manual reader, raw callback, process env, Effect.orDie, swallowed cause, Object.assign, nested runtime, method-local provision, or helper sprawl remains in the changed slice.

Verification coverage: @bundjil/channel typecheck, 2 contract tests and build passed; the agent build and 87 tests passed including 8 clean vertical tests for accepted/ignored ingress, identity rejection, stable routing, concurrent duplicate claims, dispatch success/failure, outbound acceptance/duplicate, presence, and immutable encoded state. Boundary/effect/skill checks, Knip, six-workspace typechecks, 255 workspace tests, negative scans, full verification, and git diff --check passed.

tasks.title: Build @bundjil/sendblue and replace the legacy Eve edge
tasks.description: Implement a new Sendblue ChannelTransport from protocol documentation, rewrite the Eve adapter around the clean services, replace old tests, and remove the old app-owned Sendblue implementation.
implementationNotes: Create private provider Schemas, Config input Schema, constant-time webhook verification, Effect HTTP request/response adapters, safe tagged errors, and explicit live/memory Layers without copying old app code.

Rewrite channels/sendblue.ts with one module-edge runtime, one runPromise per callback, request-scoped EveChannelDispatch Layer, waitUntil at the edge, Schema decode/encode, and one exact complete snapshot assignment.
Use new BUNDJIL_CHANNEL_SENDBLUE_*, BUNDJIL_CHANNEL_REPLAY_*, and BUNDJIL_CHANNEL_ROUTING_* config; do not read old BUNDJIL_SENDBLUE_* values or old stored state/claims.
After the new provider and app journey pass, remove apps/agent/agent/lib/sendblue/** and replace old implementation-structured tests with new contract/journey tests.
mandatoryVerification: Provider tests prove auth before body trust, body limits, valid/ignored/malformed cases, outbound encoding, complete response decoding, known rejection, timeout/uncertain delivery, presence accepted/no-op/failure, and secret-safe errors.

Direct Request/Response tests prove the new 202 accepted, 204 ignored/duplicate, 400 invalid payload, 401 authentication failure, and 503 pre-claim dependency failure semantics plus one waitUntil dispatch without importing old modules or comparing old internal state/status fixtures.

Scans in the replacement slice find no agent/lib/sendblue import, Effect.orDie, catchCause swallow, Object.assign state mutation, nested runPromise, public callback, hand-written provider attempt union, method-local dependency re-provisioning, unsafe cast, DTO mirror, manual reader, raw JSON, or helper sprawl.
Run common/Sendblue/agent checks and builds, boundary/effect/skill checks, Effect diagnostics, Knip, verification, diff checks, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: The current source tree contains only the clean Sendblue package and thin Eve adapter; the old app-owned Sendblue tree is absent.
No provider/deployment mutation occurred and the retained immutable Production deployment remains rollback truth.

tasks.title: Build @bundjil/photon and prove dual conformance locally
tasks.description: Pin the exact published Spectrum boundary, implement Photon ChannelTransport, resolve signature/resource/Space uncertainties locally, and run both providers through the same contract and app journey.
implementationNotes: Record exact npm package names, versions, integrity, exports, dependency boundary, Promise behavior, and release semantics; never target unpublished main.
Keep SDK/Zod types, client, Space, callbacks, and raw failures private; decode every unknown complete SDK result before returning a common Type.
Compare the SDK webhook helper with direct documented HMAC verification using fixed Clock/raw-body fixtures; retain only one deterministic adapter.
Use scoped Layer resource acquire/release and fault-test malformed output, rejection, timeout/uncertain send, cold Space/line ambiguity, presence no-op, and cleanup failure.
mandatoryVerification: Signed fixtures cover missing/invalid/stale/valid signatures, malformed/unsupported events, stable retry IDs, and deterministic handler completion without a callback escape hatch.
The same ChannelTransport conformance and clean app vertical suites pass with Sendblue and Photon Layers; provider selection exists only at composition roots.

Review every operation for Schema Type/Encoded boundaries, Config Schema/Redacted inputs, named Context.Service operations, scoped live/memory Layers, flat Effects, safe tagged pipe errors, and no unsafe casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers/generic callbacks/helper sprawl.
Run all package/agent checks and builds, boundary/effect/skill checks, Effect diagnostics, dependency/leak/legacy scans, verification, diff checks, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: Both providers satisfy one nominal contract without leaking provider-specific types or behavior into the app.
Any unresolved SDK lifecycle, Space, line, or uncertain-send issue stops live proof.

tasks.title: Prove the clean Photon path in an isolated Preview
tasks.description: Only with separate provider/deployment authority, configure the new Photon path in an isolated Preview and prove signed ingress, fresh replay, Eve dispatch, outbound acceptance, presence, and resource cleanup.
implementationNotes: Create/follow a target-owned runbook with exact actor, project/deployment identities, permitted operations, new config/replay namespace, rollback, rotation, evidence redaction, and stop conditions.
Use only BUNDJIL_CHANNEL_PHOTON_* and BUNDJIL_CHANNEL_REPLAY_* Preview values in an isolated Photon project; Production and Sendblue state remain unchanged.
Prove one synthetic inbound/outbound journey, stable-ID retry suppression, cold Space resolution, presence outcome, waitUntil completion, and Layer release.
Do not create/delete a dedicated line without separate authority and confirmed ambiguous-write/deletion semantics.
mandatoryVerification: Exact commit, Preview deployment, Photon project/webhook, new replay namespace, actor/authority, and rollback receipt are recorded with protected values omitted.
One signed inbound yields one fresh claim, one Eve turn, one outbound accepted result, and zero duplicate; no old state, replay key, continuation, or config value is used.
No secret, content, full identity, raw body, URL, project/message/Space ID, SDK object, prompt, tool value, or model output appears in code, logs, errors, spans, fixtures, or evidence.
Run full package/agent/build checks, boundary/effect/skill checks, Effect diagnostics, verification, diff checks, sanitized provider/runtime readback, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: An isolated Preview proves the clean Photon journey or records a terminal candidate stop with exact evidence.
No Production or Sendblue provider state changed.

tasks.title: Reconcile durable docs and define the later promotion gate
tasks.description: Update durable architecture and README owners to the verified clean implementation, close the plan, and state whether a later Production SPEC should select clean Sendblue or Photon.
implementationNotes: Update repo-structure, Eve architecture, testing, root/app/package READMEs, package tree, new config, routes, state/replay, boundary exceptions, proof, monitoring, and cutover/rollback pointers only to verified truth.
Keep completed Sendblue SPECs/plans as historical evidence and mark them non-authoritative for the replacement; do not rewrite their old proof as current behavior.
Record local and Preview proof separately and keep provider accepted distinct from handset delivered.

If promotion advances, create a new provider-selection/Production SPEC with prd-writer. It must own ingress drain, intentional continuity break, new secret binding, cutover, observation, rollback quarantine, and old-deployment retirement.
mandatoryVerification: Docs-maintainer audit accounts for every repository docs/README class: affected owners change, historical/unrelated owners are explicitly preserved or N/A, and generated audit receipts are not hand-edited.
All production/test/CLI call graphs, Type/Encoded boundaries, service/Layer exports, commands, state/replay/config claims, and proof levels match source and dated evidence.

Final source scan proves no legacy Sendblue tree/import/config/state/replay/typing/callback pattern, unsafe cast, DTO mirror, manual reader, raw JSON, deep import, raw provider client, or helper sprawl remains in the replacement.
Run all global verification, docs formatting/link/frontmatter checks, task-ledger consistency, leak/stale scans, and record every risk lens.
completionCriteria: Current docs route directly to the clean contracts, providers, operations, proof, continuity break, and promotion gate.
Photon and clean Sendblue remain non-Production until a separately accepted promotion SPEC exists.
